terms.definition: When water overflows from rivers, lakes, or the sea and covers land that is normally dry, causing damage to homes and crops.
terms.exampleUsage: The heavy rain caused a flood that covered the village roads with water.

terms.definition: A long period without rain when the land becomes very dry and there is not enough water for people, animals, and crops.
terms.exampleUsage: The drought lasted for months and the river dried up completely.

terms.definition: The rainy season in India, usually from June to September, when winds bring heavy rainfall from the sea.
terms.exampleUsage: Farmers wait for the monsoon to plant their crops.

terms.definition: Collecting and storing rainwater from rooftops and the ground so it can be used later.
terms.exampleUsage: Our school has a rainwater harvesting system on the roof.

terms.definition: A strong wall built across a river to hold back water. Dams store water and can also produce electricity.
terms.exampleUsage: The Bhakra Nangal Dam stores water from the Sutlej River.

terms.definition: The careful use of water to avoid wasting it, so there is enough water for everyone now and in the future.
terms.exampleUsage: Turning off taps when not in use is an example of water conservation.

terms.definition: An ancient Indian structure with steps leading down to the water level, used to collect and store water underground.
terms.exampleUsage: The old baoli in Rajasthan has hundreds of steps going deep into the ground.

terms.definition: A temporary shelter set up to help people who have been affected by a flood, drought, or other disaster.
terms.exampleUsage: Families moved to the relief camp after the flood destroyed their homes.

terms.definition: Water found underground in the gaps between soil and rock. Wells and tube wells bring this water up for use.
terms.exampleUsage: Farmers use groundwater from tube wells to irrigate their fields.

terms.definition: When water turns into vapour (gas) because of heat from the sun. Wet clothes dry because of evaporation.
terms.exampleUsage: Water from the puddle disappeared by evaporation when the sun came out.
